title: Thismia abei
taxonomy: Thismia abei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Balsleviaceae, a family of flowering plants endemic to Southeast Asia. It is also known to be part of the order Piperales. Further classification includes the family being a part of the clade Magnoliidae, which comprises various flowering plant families. The species was first described by the botanist Akiyo Naoki Nishida in 2007. It is considered an endangered species due to its limited geographical range and the impact of human activities on its habitat.
